description: Close integration gaps in shipped subsystems: fix tier mapping, wire OpenClaw end-to-end, start MCP server, polish BillingView, and harden CI.

userStories.title: Fix PlatformService starter tier mapping
userStories.description: As a starter-tier user, I want to see my correct tier name and credit limit so that I'm not incorrectly shown as Free/500.
acceptanceCriteria: Add case "starter": return "Starter" to PlatformService.tierDisplayName(_:)
Add case "starter": return 2000 to the file-level tierCreditsPerMonth(_:) function
Existing free, pro, team mappings unchanged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Restore OpenClawService settings from UserDefaults on init
userStories.description: As a user, I want my OpenClaw settings (isEnabled, gatewayURL) to persist across app restarts so I don't have to reconfigure every launch.
acceptanceCriteria: Add UserDefaults persistence keys (e.g. OpenClaw_Enabled, OpenClaw_GatewayURL)
In init(), read saved values from UserDefaults falling back to current defaults
Add didSet observers on isEnabled and gatewayURL that write to UserDefaults
Follow same pattern as OpenClawCostControl (lines 23-35, 61-93)
Setting isEnabled = true persists across app restart
Changing gatewayURL persists across app restart
Default values are false and ws://127.0.0.1:18789 for fresh installs
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix OpenClawCostControl.currentPolicy() hardcoded values
userStories.description: As a user, I want the cost policy sent to the OpenClaw gateway to reflect my actual configured limits, not hardcoded defaults.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove nonisolated from currentPolicy() so it can access actor-isolated @Published properties
Replace four hardcoded values with perSessionCreditCap, perDayCreditCap, requestsPerMinute, requireOwnAPIKey
Update call sites that call currentPolicy() from non-isolated context to use await
currentPolicy() returns user's actual configured values not hardcoded defaults
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire OpenClaw notification into ChatViewModel
userStories.description: As a developer using OpenClaw, I want session messages from the gateway to be routed through ChatViewModel's streaming pipeline so the OpenClaw flow actually works end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: ChatViewModel subscribes to .openClawMessageReceived notification
When notification fires, extract sessionId, content, and optional model from userInfo
Create new conversation or append to active one then call existing streaming pipeline
After streaming completes call OpenClawService.shared.sendResponse(sessionId:content:done:) with final response
If streaming fails send error response back with done: true
Add separate handleOpenClawMessage method (do not restructure existing sendMessage flow)
Normal user-initiated chat is unaffected
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 and US-003 being completed first

userStories.title: Track messageCount and creditsUsed on OpenClawSession
userStories.description: As a user monitoring OpenClaw sessions, I want to see accurate message and credit counts instead of 0/0.
acceptanceCriteria: In handleSessionMessage after cost control check passes, find session in activeSessions by sessionId and increment messageCount
Also increment creditsUsed by the credit amount passed to costControl.messageProcessed() (default 1.0)
Use firstIndex(where:) and mutate in place since OpenClawSession is a struct in an array
activeSessions mutation triggers @Published update for UI
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 being completed first

userStories.title: Start MCPServerHost behind AppStorage toggle
userStories.description: As a user, I want to enable the MCP server via a Settings toggle so that external MCP clients can connect to G-Rump.
acceptanceCriteria: Add @AppStorage("EnableMCPServer") private var enableMCPServer = false to GRumpApp
After splash dismissal in macOS section, start MCP server via Task if enableMCPServer is true
Add onChange(of: enableMCPServer) modifier to start/stop server at runtime
When EnableMCPServer is true and app launches MCPServerHost starts on port 18790
When toggle is flipped off MCPServerHost stops
Default is off, no server starts
App startup not blocked by MCP server init (uses Task)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement 6 missing MCPToolDispatcher tools
userStories.description: As an MCP client user, I want all 13 advertised tools to actually work instead of 6 returning errors.
acceptanceCriteria: edit_file: reads file, finds old_text, replaces with new_text, writes back, errors if old_text not found
search_files: uses FileManager to enumerate directory and filter by pattern, returns matching paths
grep_search: uses Process to run /usr/bin/grep -rn with pattern and directory, returns output
web_search: returns clear 'Web search requires interactive mode' message not silent error
read_url: uses URLSession.shared.data(from:) with 10-second timeout, returns body text
clipboard_write: uses NSPasteboard.general.setString to write text, returns confirmation
No tool silently falls through to default error case
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-006 being completed first

userStories.title: Add BillingView error alerts and loading spinner
userStories.description: As a user viewing billing info, I want to see loading indicators and error messages instead of a blank page when the backend is unreachable.
acceptanceCriteria: ProgressView overlay or inline indicator shows when isLoading is true
Alert or inline error banner shows when errorMessage is non-nil
In loadBillingData replace try? with do/catch setting errorMessage on failure
In openCheckout add error handling setting errorMessage if network call fails or no checkout URL
In openPortal add similar error handling
Retry button available in error state that calls loadBillingData again
Error messages can be dismissed
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix CI: print() scan recursion and SwiftLint blocking
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CI to catch print statements in subdirectories and fail on lint errors instead of silently passing.
acceptanceCriteria: print() check uses recursive search covering Sources/GRump/Views/ and other subdirectories
SwiftLint command no longer has || true so lint failures cause CI to fail
Keep outer if command -v check for SwiftLint availability
Story documents that backend/stripe.js must be tracked in git (do not execute git add)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add backend billing route tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want test coverage for billing routes and Stripe integration constants so regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Add describe('Billing & Stripe') section to backend/tests/backend.test.js
Test getUsageAnalytics(userId): create user, insert usage_log rows, verify correct aggregates
Test CREDIT_PACKS has entries for credits_1000, credits_5000, credits_20000
Test PRICE_IDS contains all 6 price keys
Test TIER_MAP maps starter_monthly to starter, pro_monthly to pro, team_monthly to team
No tests require Stripe API key or network access
cd backend && npm test passes
Tests pass
